Q1 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider a relation S = (A, B, C, D). For which of the following sets of FD’s in S is not in 3rd normal form?

Correct answer: B, C
Solution

(d)
For (i): For FD A → D, the LHS is not the super key but RHS is a prime attribute. Therefore, it is
in 3NF.
For (ii): The candidate keys are AD, BD and CD. Thus, for FD BC → A, the LHS is not the
superkey but RHS is a prime attribute. Therefore, it is in 3NF.
For (iii), The candidate keys are AC and D. Thus, all the FD's satisfy the 3NF condition and thus
it is in 3NF.
For (iv), the candidate key is C, therefore, FD AC → B, A → D, D → B neither LHS is the super
key nor RHS is having the prime attribute, therefore, it is not in 3NF.

Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Which of the following correctly matches the levels of data abstraction in DBMS?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
• External level: User view
• Conceptual level: Logical design, how information is connected
• Internal level: How data is actually stored

Q4 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the given relation Student(SID, Name, Age) where not all students age known. What
does the following SQL query returns?
SELECT COUNT(∗) FROM Student WHERE Age < > 20;

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
NULL < > 20 is unknown, so such rows are not selected.
SQL ignores NULLs in inequality comparisons unless explicitly handled.

Q5 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Consider two relations Department and Employee:
Dept(DID, DName)
Emp(EID, EName, DID)
Where DID and EID are the primary key in Dept and Emp relation respectively. Assume DID in Emp referencing to Dept DID.
Which of the following operations will not violate referential integrity.

Correct answer: A, B
Solution

(a, b)
(a) Violates primary key constraint if DID = 5 exist.
(b) Does not affect Dept and Emp relations.
(c) May violates if DID referenced by Employee.
(d) May violates if no DID exist in Department.

Q6 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +2 / -0
Not attempted

A hospital database stores information about doctors, patients and treatments. Each doctor has a unique ID and a name. Each patient has a unique ID and a set of phone number. A doctor can treat multiple patients and each treatment records the date and diagnosis. A patient can be treated by multiple doctors over time.
Which of the following ER-diagram correctly represents the given information.

Correct answer: A, D
Solution

(a, d)
• Option (b): False : Not all patient done treatment, phone no. is a multivalued attribute.
• Option (c): False: Not all doctors done treatment.

Q9 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statements is/are TRUE about weak entity set?

Correct answer: A, B, C
Solution

(a, b, c)
Weak entities can be deleted automatically when their strong entity is deleted. Correct
Weak entity set avoids the data duplication and consequent possible inconsistencies caused by
duplicating the key of the strong entity. Correct
A weak entity set has no primary keys unless attributes of the strong entity on which it depends
are included. Correct
Tuples in a weak entity set are not partitioned according to their relationship with tuples in a
strong entity set. This is Incorrect, because tuples in a weak entity set are partitioned according to
their relationship with tuples in a strong entity set.

Q17 NAT Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Consider the following B+ tree with the order of internal and leaf nodes as 3 and 2 respectively:

 
The minimum number of key insertions that causes a new level to be introduced in the above B+ tree ________. (Assume key redistribution is not allowed)
NAT
Correct answer: 3
Solution

3
If we insert keys 45,48,55 in same order, then on insertion of key 55, root will be overflow and new level will be created.

Q26 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider following 2 queries with relation schema :

 
 
Which of the following query results “all students who never got a grade above 4.0”?
Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• Query 1 uses a correlated subquery with NOT EXISTS, implementing an anti-join to exclude
students having any course with Grade > 4.0. It also includes student with no enrollment
records. Since, no matching tuple exist in course.
• Query 2 performs a set difference between all student.ID S and those in course with grade > 4.0,
followed by a join with student to get complete records. This also retains student who never
enrolled in any course.
So, both queries return the same result.

Q33 NAT Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Assume there are 10 transaction : T1, T2, ... T10. Every transaction writes a value of variable X that is read by its successive transaction, like T1 writes a value of X that is read by T2, T2 writes a value of X, that is read by T3, ..., T9 writes a value of X that is read by T10. Now, if T1 fails, then total number of transactions required to recover the schedule are __________.

NAT
Correct answer: 10
Solution

(10)
In order to recover the schedule, all the 10 transactions are required as the successive transaction is dependent on the data updated by previous transaction like T2 is dependent on a variable which is updated by T1. As T1 fails, T2 is also required to be recovered. Now if T2 is recovered, T3 is also recovered as T3 depends on data updated by T2. Likewise, it happens for all the transactions till T10.
